title: The Recurrence Death
concept: RNNs process sequentially, creating a training speed bottleneck; self-attention computes relationships for all tokens simultaneously, unlocking GPU parallelization.

exercises.prompt: Why do traditional RNNs train slowly on modern computer hardware?

options.text: They are too small to fit on modern GPUs.
They process words one-by-one, creating a waiting line.
They only work on CPUs and cannot run on GPUs at all.

exercises.explain: RNNs process tokens sequentially (one after another), which prevents GPUs from processing data in parallel and slows down training.

cards.title: The Speed Bottleneck
cards.body:
Before Transformers took over the world, AI models called RNNs had a huge speed problem. They read text word-by-word, like a human reader. 

This made training them on giant datasets incredibly slow. Let's find out how we broke this speed limit!

cards.title: Breaking the Chain
cards.body:
The Recurrence Death refers to the slow, step-by-step nature of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) that prevents them from running in parallel. 

Self-attention solves this by looking at all words in a sentence at the same time, allowing computers to process massive amounts of text simultaneously.

cards.title: The Bucket Brigade Bottleneck
cards.body:
Think of an RNN like a bucket brigade where people pass water buckets one by one down a long line. You cannot pass the fifth bucket until the fourth person has handed it over. 

Self-attention is like a modern sprinkler system that waters the entire field at the exact same time. No waiting in line required!

cards.title: Sequential vs. Simultaneous
cards.body:
Imagine translating the sentence: "The dog chased the cat."

An RNN reads: "The", then "dog", then "chased", and so on, waiting for each step to finish.

A Self-Attention model looks at ["The", "dog", "chased", "the", "cat"] all at once, calculating how every word connects to the others in one single giant leap.

cards.title: Do We Need to Read in Order?
cards.body:
You might think that reading text word-by-word is the only way for an AI to understand language order. 

In reality, processing all words at once (parallelization) while using position markers actually helps the AI learn language patterns much faster and more accurately.

cards.title: Sequential vs. Parallel
cards.body: Compare how the two architectures handle a sequence of words. RNNs queue up in a line, while Self-Attention handles everyone together.

left.text: Processes 'Word 1', waits, then 'Word 2', waits, then 'Word 3'. Slow bottleneck.

right.text: Processes 'Word 1', 'Word 2', and 'Word 3' all at the exact same time. Super fast!

exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of RNNs that "The Recurrence Death" refers to?

options.text: They forget the beginning of the sentence instantly.
They must process tokens one-by-one, creating a speed bottleneck.
They are too simple to understand grammar.
They only work with numbers, not words.

exercises.explain: RNNs must process words one after another (sequentially), which creates a bottleneck because we cannot utilize modern GPU parallel processing.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Self-attention enables models to process all words in a sentence simultaneously.
exercises.explain: Self-attention allows computers to process all tokens at the same time, unlocking the true parallel processing power of GPUs.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about training efficiency.
exercises.sentence: RNNs process tokens one after another, whereas self-attention allows us to process them in ________.
exercises.explain: RNNs force computers to work sequentially, which wastes the power of modern GPUs. Self-attention allows parallel processing, making training incredibly fast.

exercises.prompt:
Identify which statement is correct about self-attention and RNNs.

Human: "Self-attention models process tokens sequentially, while RNNs process them all at once."
AI: "RNNs process tokens one-by-one, while self-attention processes all tokens at the same time."
exercises.explain: The AI statement is incorrect because self-attention processes tokens simultaneously (in parallel), which is why it trains so much faster than sequential RNNs.

exercises.prompt: Why do models using self-attention train so much faster on GPUs than traditional RNNs?

options.text: They require less memory to store weights.
They process all words at the same time instead of step-by-step.
They use simpler mathematical calculations than RNNs.
They only process one word at a time, making it safer.

exercises.explain: Because RNNs calculate step-by-step, we cannot calculate step 100 without calculating the previous 99 steps first. Self-attention breaks this chain by calculating all connections instantly in parallel, maximizing the power of GPUs.
